# UC-10.16.7 · Risk Score Distribution Trending

## Description

A shifting histogram of Enterprise Security risk scores shows whether the environment is accumulating unmanaged risk objects or whether tuning and closure activities are working. Persistent high-score tails warrant entity prioritization and threat-hunting focus.

## Value

A shifting histogram of Enterprise Security risk scores shows whether the environment is accumulating unmanaged risk objects or whether tuning and closure activities are working. Persistent high-score tails warrant entity prioritization and threat-hunting focus.

## Implementation

Ensure `risk_score` reflects the consolidated object score post-RBA aggregation. Filter to active risk objects if you archive stale entries. Compare week-over-week using `timewrap` on bucketed counts. Feed executive dashboards with simplified bands; keep raw drilldown for analysts in ES Risk Analysis.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=risk`, Risk data model.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ensure `risk_score` reflects the consolidated object score post-RBA aggregation. Filter to active risk objects if you archive stale entries. Compare week-over-week using `timewrap` on bucketed counts. Feed executive dashboards with simplified bands; keep raw drilldown for analysts in ES Risk Analysis.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=risk earliest=-90d
| eval band=case(risk_score<20,"0-19", risk_score<40,"20-39", risk_score<60,"40-59", risk_score<80,"60-79", true(),"80-100")
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count by _time, band
| xyseries _time band count
| fillnull value=0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Risk Score Distribution Trending** — A shifting histogram of Enterprise Security risk scores shows whether the environment is accumulating unmanaged risk objects or whether tuning and closure activities are working. Persistent high-score tails warrant entity prioritization and threat-hunting focus.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=risk`, Risk data model. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: risk.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=risk, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **band** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, band** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pivots fields for charting with `xyseries`.
• Fills null values with `fillnull`.


Step 3 — Validate
Drill from the chart into a few high-risk **entities** in ES or your risk model and ensure the same scores appear in the underlying events.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked column chart (count by band over weeks), heatmap (band × week), line chart (80–100 band trend).


## SPL

```spl
index=risk earliest=-90d
| eval band=case(risk_score<20,"0-19", risk_score<40,"20-39", risk_score<60,"40-59", risk_score<80,"60-79", true(),"80-100")
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count by _time, band
| xyseries _time band count
| fillnull value=0
```

## Visualization

Stacked column chart (count by band over weeks), heatmap (band × week), line chart (80–100 band trend).
